channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden will survey Hurricane Milton damage in Florida, Harris attends church in North Carolina
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday will survey the devastation inflicted on Florida's Gulf Coast by Hurricane Milton as he urges Congress to approve additional emergency disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What Scott Peterson's recent court win could mean for his yearslong quest for freedom
item.description: A California judge has granted convicted murderer Scott Peterson's request for discovery in his case after the Los Angeles Innocence Project took up his case in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Determination to rebuild follows Florida's hurricanes with acceptance that storms will come again
item.description: VENICE, Fla. (AP) — No sooner had residents of the Bahia Vista Gulf condominium complex dug out and from Hurricane Helene than they were faced with the same daunting cleanup from new damage inflicted by Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY O'NEIL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump hits Harris on immigration in her home state of California
item.description: Former President Trump slammed Vice President Harris in her native California on Saturday evening, ranting about migrants and bashing illegal immigration in the Golden State.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Rising chocolate prices impact Halloween candy choices
item.description: Despite rising chocolate and candy prices, Americans are expected to spend $3.5 million on Halloween sweets this year.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, ABC 4

item.title: Which states have the highest and lowest voter turnout in presidential elections?
item.description: The rate of voter turnout for the 2020 presidential election was around 67%. But some states were really bringing up that average.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Ronaldo scores again in Nations League win for perfect Portugal. Spain finds replacement for Rodri
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo scored his record-extending 133rd international goal to keep Portugal perfect in the Nations League with a 3-1 win over Poland on Saturday, and Spain struck late to edge Denmark 1-0.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Far from where Hurricane Milton's eye hit, tornadoes wrought unexpected damage
item.description: WELLINGTON, Fla. (AP) — Tony Brazzale, a diving boat captain who has lived for 10 years in his Wellington home in southeastern Florida, wasn't worried about Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Virginia woman, 27, chronicles fight against cancer on TikTok
item.description: Caldwell and her fiancée, Tyler, were just months from tying the knot when she felt one in her breast.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Hudson, NewsNation

item.title: Historic ocean liner could soon become the world's largest artificial reef
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The conservancy that oversees a storied but aging ocean liner and its landlord have resolved a years-old rent dispute that will clear the way for a Florida county to turn the historic ship into the world’s largest artificial reef.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Inspectors find bed bugs in Las Vegas Bellagio, Cosmopolitan hotels
item.description: A report reveals health inspectors found bed bugs at some prominent hotels on the Las Vegas Strip during a six-month period.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, NewsNation

item.title: Harris outpaces Trump in Pennsylvania, but trails in Arizona: Poll
item.description: The New York Times/Philadelphia Inquirer/Siena College survey, released Saturday, shows Harris leading Trump 50 percent to 47 percent among likely voters in the Keystone State.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Harris outpaces Trump in battleground Pennsylvania, but trails in Arizona: Poll
item.description: The New York Times/Philadelphia Inquirer/Siena College survey, released Saturday, shows Harris leading Trump 50 percent to 47 percent among likely voters in the Keystone State.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Inspectors find bed bugs at Bellagio, Cosmopolitan hotels in Las Vegas
item.description: A report reveals health inspectors found bed bugs at some prominent hotels on the Las Vegas Strip during a six-month period.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How schools are tackling misinformation as false claims about hurricanes, election run wild
item.description: Misinformation generally has been an increasing concern, most recently with how the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) has been responding to the aftermath of Hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Visitor finds 2.3-carat diamond at Arkansas state park
item.description: A visitor to the Arkansas Crater of Diamonds State Park left with a 2.3-carat diamond to commemorate the trip.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, Fox 5

item.title: UCLA professor says he’s homeless due to low pay
item.description: (KTLA) -- In a now-viral TikTok video, Dr. Daniel McKeown, a professor at UCLA, claims low pay has left him homeless, shocking many of his followers. "Hi everyone, my name is Daniel, and I’m an astrophysics professor at UCLA.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, Fox 5

item.title: Whooping cough cases surge to record levels in US, CDC says
item.description: Whooping cough cases have increased to record levels since 2014, with four times as many cases compared to last year, data from the CDC showed. There have been more than 16,000 cases this year alone and two confirmed deaths, statistics revealed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida deputies help pregnant woman in labor during Milton
item.description: Polk County Sheriff Grady Judd said that some of the office's deputies helped transport a pregnant woman who was going into labor to the hospital early Thursday morning, as Hurricane Milton brought widespread power outages, high winds and flooding to Central Florida.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, WGN 9

item.title: Ohio mother of 8-year-old girl who took family’s SUV for Target run said it was 'impressive' but 'dangerous'
item.description: The Ohio mother of the eight-year-old girl who drove herself and her family's dog in her family's SUV to Target said she found her daughter's driving ability "impressive.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Catholic League accuses Whitmer of mocking Eucharist with Dorito
item.description: Podcaster Liz Plank posted a video on her Instagram showing herself being fed a Dorito by Whitmer, who's standing above Plank.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WGN 9

item.title: Fidelity data breach exposed info from 77,000 customers: Was your account compromised?
item.description: Thousands of Fidelity Investments customers may have had their personal information stolen amid a data breach that occurred in August of this year, the financial firm announced.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Experts warn 'crazy busy' Atlantic hurricane season is far from over
item.description: Millions of people in the southeastern U.S. still are reeling from the catastrophic damage caused by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, but scientists warn that the Atlantic hurricane season is far from over. “As far as hurricane landfalls in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Climate change gave significant boost to Milton's destructive power, scientists say
item.description: Researchers said that without climate change, a hurricane like Milton would make landfall as a weaker Category 2, not considered a “major” storm, instead of a Category 3.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Climate change gave significant boost to Milton's destructive rain, winds, scientists say
item.description: Human-caused climate change intensified deadly Hurricane Milton 's rainfall by 20 to 30% and strengthened its winds by about 10%, scientists said in a new flash study.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This winter may be less predictable than the last few. Here's why
item.description: This winter was originally thought to be dominated by the La Niña phenomenon. But now, the Climate Prediction Center believes it will be a "weak and a short duration La Niña" – if it appears at all.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: 1 killed, 23 rescued after equipment malfunction at tourist gold mine in Colorado
item.description: One person was killed and 23 were rescued after being trapped for about six hours inside a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KRON4

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, pick up debris after Milton
item.description: Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 1 person dies and 12 are rescued after elevator malfunctions at Colorado gold mine tourist site
item.description: DENVER (AP) — One person was killed and 12 people were rescued after being trapped for about six hours at the bottom of a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, social activist and wife of Robert F. Kennedy, has died
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ethel Kennedy, the wife of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy, who raised their 11 children after he was assassinated and remained dedicated to social causes and the family’s legacy for decades thereafter, died on Thursday, her family said. She was 96.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tampa sees 5x the usual October rainfall due to Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton's landfall as a Category 3 hurricane didn't just bring devastating storm surge and extremely dangerous wind speeds, it also left Tampa Bay soaked with historic rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutors reviewing 'new evidence' in Marilyn Manson sexual abuse probe
item.description: The sex crimes division of the Los Angeles County District Attorney’s Office is reviewing new leads and evidence in the investigation into shock-rocker Marilyn Manson, who has been accused by multiple women, including “Westworld” actor Evan Rachel Wood, of sexual abuse, officials announced Wednesday
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago man charged with assaulting two officers during protests of Netanyahu address to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Illinois man was arrested Thursday on charges that he attacked two police officers during protests in Washington, D.C., against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s address to Congress in July.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Milton’s winds topple crane that was building west Florida’s tallest residential tower
item.description: The mayor in St. Petersburg, Florida, warned residents that cranes at several construction projects across the city might fall in Hurricane Milton and at the storm's peak on Wednesday night, one came crashing down.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 14-year-old found floating on fence in Tampa floodwaters after Hurricane Milton
item.description: A 14-year-old had to be rescued after Hurricane Milton's floodwaters trapped him on a floating fence in the middle of Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Atlantic City mayor and his wife plead not guilty to beating their daughter
item.description: MAYS LANDING, N.J. (AP) — The mayor of Atlantic City and his wife, who is the seaside gambling resort's schools superintendent, pleaded not guilty Thursday to beating and abusing their teenage daughter, with a lawyer saying that “parenting struggles are not criminal events.” Mayor Marty Small Sr.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Social Security announces 2.5 percent cost-of-living adjustment for 2025
item.description: Social Security recipients will see a 2.5 percent bump as part of their cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2025.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, ABC 4
